content: The incomplete, iterative search-based knowledge building process that defines both topics can be reframed as a curiosity-driven active learning mechanism itself: the fragmented search traces of developing DeepResearch Agents are not flaws, but the natural emergent memory traces of a meta-research agent that learns to improve its own research capabilities through unresolved uncertainty. Just as curiosity-driven reinforcement learning uses prediction error to drive exploration, the missing chunks and repeated search queries in these knowledge outlines act as intrinsic reward signals to prioritize further inquiry into the most uncertain gaps in agent architecture.
